item.title: Re: ;-;
item.content:encoded: Man, I totally forgot I wrote that little comment tale. Maybe one day cookie!test room will be on the mainsite.

aaaaahh tragicomedy indeed
And then i read zyns "tale" in the comments, it's even more heartbreaking
D=

item.title: Re: one word change to improve on this
item.content:encoded: 'unwished' fits better with the character's idiolect.

item.title: one word change to improve on this
item.content:encoded:
Love it but I think it could be improved slightly by modifying what the guy's wish is to remove the possibility of a successful detox because obviously a fully functional genie who's eternally grateful to the Foundation is too OP.
"the first wish couldn't, couldn't be unwished"
If "unwished" was changed to "undone" there it would imply that the genie's condition couldn't be changed by any means, rather than only unchangeable by another wish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The secret to unlimited energy should be a deviled egg, even though in all likelihood it actually isn't. Those things are tasty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually thought of a way of "undoing" its tragedy that can still make for a good story and character, but I don't think I can pull it out myself.

The addiction of this genie can't be undone by means of a wish, but it isn't said that it can't go through rehab. Basically, the wish was for it to be a junkie, but not to stay that way forever. A story of this character getting over drugs the old fashioned way could be very interesting, and still have some lasting consequences for this period.

But what actually bothers me a little is the possibility of this genie not having a limit to its powers, outside its state of mind. I know it isn't stated as being all powerful, but it didn't went well for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The problem with fixing him is then you have a grateful genie who is functional and has a motivation to grant you wishes. WAY too useful sadly. I LOVE this story. It's sad on so many levels, what kind of sadistic person would do that. I would wish that that person was trapped, eternally alive and at the genies access to be tortured whenever for all eternity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I pity this poor guy but I do think there is a way to undo his tragedy which I intend to write a tale about since this character deserves to be expanded upon.

Still the quality was great and how it pulls the heart strings as it progressed was astounding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I may not have written any stories/tales myself, but as Zyn wrote earlier in the discussion page, I think it would be Amazing to have a collab page about wishes with this guy. Either way, I am not saying that the article is bad for not having that as a supplement page, i'm just saying that I think that would be say, "fun."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shit, I'm amazed how quickly it stopped being funny and started being extremely sad. Poor thing.

item.title: subtle detail that I'm pleased with
item.content:encoded:
so there I, I, there I was, right in f-f-f-front of this guy, waiting for him to make his, his fuckin' wishes an', an', an', he looks at me an' he says 'for my f-f-f-first wish, I, I, I,… for my…' He says… he… he fuckin' wishes for me to be a fuckin' alcoholic j-j-j-junkie tweaker crackhead!

that passage right there, where the genie doesn't just stutter and stammer but actually has to keep rephrasing before he can continue… that's where the guy said "I wish for you to be an alcoholic junkie tweaker crackhead".

Note that the genie has to describe what the guy said, instead of repeating it. This is because the genie is unable to say the words 'I wish'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An' then he says that his, his second, his second wish is that the, the, the first wish couldn't, couldn't be unwished.

Then just unwish the second wish! But seriously, that guy who made those wishes is an asshole douchebag. Nevertheless, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor fucking bastard +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted this a long time ago, and I finally checked the discussion to read everything and maybe suggest a way to fix this poor thing, only to find that the thread had already covered my idea, many more, and just in general made everything somehow even sadder. Honestly at this point, I would just wish for him to not exist and end his misery, assuming the cessation of existence doesn't count as a benefit and his powers let him, and also assuming he can string together enough lucid thoughts from the tangled mess of crack and alcohol to actually do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sympathies for the djinn, but I have a feeling that The Foundation would still try and 'train' it.

rewarding it with drugs and food for correct wishes, etc.

who's a good boy?
Sit,
roll over,
give us a method of easily renewable, low danger and pollution power.

GOOD BOY! *tosses djinn a cocaine sammich*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You are my hero Voct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After the first wish was made, the genie had a few seconds before his ichorstream became totally flooded with alcohol and etc. During those few seconds, it was hypothetically possible for someone to undo the first wish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then what was the point of having the second wish be made in the first place?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But why can't the second wish be unwished, allowing the first wish to be unwished.

Because the genie is in such a terrible state that he probably couldn't grant the unwishing of the second wish, even if he tried.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so the first wish can't be unwished because the second wish said that the first wish can't be unwished.

But why can't the second wish be unwished, allowing the first wish to be unwished.

item.title: I've finally made it to the 100+ Club
item.content:encoded: Everybody take their clothes off

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: aww. thats super sad :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the story of the saddest genie who ever lived. Prepare your feels
+1

item.title: Re: -1
item.content:encoded: Honesty is better than nothing! Thanks for the kind words!

I think this is an interesting concept, and the hook is well placed. The writing gets the job done with room to spare… But this didn't interest me one way or another. It's not because I don't like character driven skips; this would have more room to breathe as a Wanderer's tale than a Foundation skip. If this was the first thing I read on the site I would probably not be interested enough to read more. The story and the tone might feel like two separate entities. I also cannot in good conscience upvote a crackhead genie, but I look forward to more from you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, this poor guy. :(

This is very compelling. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much :D that means a lot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have any idea how I missed this one. This is one of the most entertaining, flavorful, and fun SCPs I've read in a while. Nicely done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The most obscure unintentional self insert on the entire site :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Guy used to be a beautiful flower, now he's an ugly flower.

It's sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing is, all these stories that you are referring to involve the genie being set free through a wish. As Voct mentioned, the genie's wish granting skills are not very accurate. Even if someone had wished for its freedom, it is doubtful that anything besides a ham sandwich would come out of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He's not allowed to benefit from his own powers. He can benefit from the power of someone else's wish.

Semantics, really. He's not allowed to do this stuff of his own volition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some stories have the master using their third wish to wish for the genies freedom, demonstrating the nobility of the wisher. Others have had masters who wish for strength, money and power turned into genies themselves, freeing the original genie by taking their place in the bottle. The prohibition seems to be against genies making the wishes themselves, not against benefitting from them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also.

The reason he can't produce drugs for himself is that he's not able to use his powers for his own benefit — as is the case with all genies.

It's also why he doesn't generate food for himself whenever he wants — only when someone wishes for food (or makes a complicated wish when he happens to be hungry or otherwise distracted).

The weeping is because he's just been forcibly reminded of the fact that he's eating the food himself and thus is being a BAD genie. BAD! BAD! NO! BAD! NO EAT! BAD GENIE! NO EAT! NO!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to include it so much in the report, but there is no way I could get away with it :'(

Late reply on my part, but haha, thanks. xD

It's way more of a tale than a test log, though. And it's very much tongue-in-cheek. I really like this article, though, and writing the Mark and Riven dialogue was fun.

Maybe a short collection of Foundation vignettes… collab tale featuring the crackhead genie and assorted characters, anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
why not have the foundation put in place measures to ensure he doesn't rehabilitate himself?

That would count as "unwishing the first wish", I think, since it would involve "doing something that is what he himself wants".

that is a ridiculous amount of meth, does he have genie drug tolerance

Yes.

Note that he asked for just thirty or forty grams, as if that's a tiny tiny tiny amount for him. Also realize how much it takes to keep him from going through withdrawal all the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be honest I quite like the idea of the foundation attempting to rehabilitate him, I doubt even the foundation would turn down unlimited wishes. That being said, if the general consensus is the opposite then why not have the foundation put in place measures to ensure he doesn't rehabilitate himself?

Also, that is a ridiculous amount of meth, does he have genie drug tolerance or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep. My assumption is that the wish would be something that the djinn wants to bring about, so he'd be more inclined to adhere to the spirit rather than the letter for this specific wish. If he's content as he is, obviously that's unlikely, but he comes across as more "at rock bottom" than "content".

And yeah, that's what got me thinking about it in the first place. A version of that kind of acknowledgement is inherent in the Master/Djinn relationship, that's often strongly implied or outright stated to be the reason that they twist wishes. Also, someone bound him to that mug, and someone made him an addict. Precedent definitely exists in his frame of reference for him to acknowledge his powerlessness. He may not like it, but he knows he's not in control.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this was awesome lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First - his track record with granting wishes isn't that great. You wish for something specific, you'll be lucky to get something remotely like it.

Second - did you know twelve-step programs tend to include a step about 'acknowledging a greater power'? How's a genie going to feel about that? Especially if he does manage to get clean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They could wish for him to engage in, follow and faithfully uphold an effective 12-step program. An addict in recovery is still an addict, so it wouldn't counter the already made wishes. Wish-granters are notorious for following the 'letter' of wishes rather than the spirit, after all.

*shrug*

No vote for now. For me it reads like a padded one-sentence concept for an SCP rather than a developed idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, if you had read the divine law book of genies you would have known that.
..
SOME PEOPLE ARE JUST SO LAZY.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Curious, if the guy who wished that his wish that made the genie became a drug addict could not be unwished, couldn't somebody unwish the man's second wish to unwish the first wish?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The experiment with the deviled egg… I luf this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made me laugh really hard, then made me sad.
Good work, Voct, Flower. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
I choked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love you. This is amazing ahah! Oh how I do wish this was an actual thing. I want to include it so much in the report, but there is no way I could get away with it :'(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fanmade dedicated tale, you say?
I REGRET NOTHINNGGGG

Doctor Mark Kiryu frowned as he stared through the experimental room's outer observation window, completely ignoring his assistant Riven as he passed by, arms laden with assorted paperwork.

"Mark, you shouldn't scowl. It'll give you early wrinkles, and I don't think your facial muscles are accustomed to it anyway. You look like you're trying to swallow a lemon." Riven paused, shifting the papers so he wouldn't drop them. "Come on, let's move the SCP back to containment. Site security's spread thin enough as is, and we shouldn't need that many guards for this one."

Dr. Kiryu didn't acknowledge the comment. "Riven, did Zyn's routine maintenance checkup schedule include SCP-1481's containment unit?"

Riven glanced at a chart hanging by the examination room. "Hm, yes, it did. Your sister—"

"…should be removed from 1481 duty," Kiryu muttered. "What does today's log say?"

Checking a clipboard hanging near the door to the room, Riven read aloud, "manifested half a rubber duck, a set of bongos when asked for a drum set, a towel, and… huh. Apparently the researcher accidentally muttered something and SCP-1481 heard it."

"Do tell," Mark remarked dryly.

"Something about wishing that 1481 could just have a cookie." Riven read in bewilderment.

"Ah. That would explain the state of the testing room." Mark gestured to the window. Riven peered into the room, and immediately noticed that the humanoid within appeared to be gnawing on the floor with obvious relish. Test chamber floors were, as with most things Foundation, standardized, sterilized, and plain. The test floor in the particular chamber appeared to be made entirely of golden-brown doughy goodness scattered with brick-sized chocolate chips.

Mark sighed. "I suppose that qualifies as "a cookie", albeit a rather large one. Imagine how long it'd take to clean this up…" He tugged absentmindedly on the collar of his turtleneck. "Poor Zyn. I can understand her feeling sorry for it, but I'm afraid I can no longer overlook repeated infractions."

"I don't know, we've seen worse, and he looks pretty happy to me," Riven commented as he and the doctor watched the spectral humanoid proceed to roll around the cookie-padded floor in delirious glee.

"This experimental chamber is now completely useless."

"No, it's not. Maybe we can keep the arms in there if tissue storage runs out of room."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Psst* fan made dedicated tale *nudge nudge*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay. I wish that this was on the main article, but at the same time it would ruin tone and atmosphere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With the changes you've made, I'm not getting any hesitation /bother feelings anymore. Well done on a great SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As Researcher Zyn observed the pathetic genie through the window of the test chamber, she could not help but tear up as she watched SCP-1481-1 shuddering in the corner, recovering from the effects as the hallucinations of methamphetamine wore off. Researcher Zyn sighed to herself.

"I wish for a thousand hugs for this guy," she muttered under her breath.

SCP-1481-1 has been crushed to death by a sudden manifestation of approximately 2000 dismembered arms, filling up Test Chamber 37. Corpse de-manifested in Medical Ward 14 approximately 2 hours after death. When SCP-1481 was rubbed again, SCP-1481 re-manifested, apparently unharmed but unaware of the events following its death, stating "just saw a crapton o' arms appear an' thought I were still trippin'". Further investigation needed.
Arms deemed unanomalous, stored in Site-105's tissue storage chambers, available for all personnel upon request.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I feel bad for this guy. For some reason I found the genie's eating of all the food he manifests charming in an adorable, pathetic sort of way.

Let me hug plz

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Poor Hobo Genie. :( That last interview made me feel so bad. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Homeless drug addicts are supposed to be played for laughs?

I liked this because "holy shit that's tragic" was the first thing to pop in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, that's a good point. I was thinking it must've been an act of revenge, but if this is a Jafar-esque power genie… yeah. Good work, to the both of you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can't kill a genie!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just because the Foundation ain't cruel, that doesn't mean they ain't cold.

Someone might have had a very good reason for making this genie into an ineffective junkie. Do we want to risk undoing that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Voct worked on tonal issues, the accent of SCP-1481-1 and removed some lines here and there. Thank you! The final line of the report was "Research towards rehabilitating SCP-1481-1 is underway." Voct convinced me it wasn't appropriate as genies are quite powerful and a sober genie with freewill could probably wreak havoc on the foundation.
